initiativeCore.problemStatement: Users lack a seamless, accessible way to track daily habits and personal goals with timely reminders and visual progress tracking across multiple communication channels.
goals: Enable users to track daily habits and personal goals
Provide customizable reminders to support habit consistency
Visualize habit-building progress through intuitive charts
Deliver streak notifications to motivate continued engagement
Integrate with SMS, Telegram, and WhatsApp for multi-channel accessibility (one channel per user, selected at signup)
Support bi-directional communication allowing users to mark habits complete via messaging platforms
nonGoals: Social features or habit sharing between users (not in initial scope)
Advanced analytics or AI-powered habit recommendations (not in initial scope)
Integration with additional messaging platforms beyond SMS, Telegram, and WhatsApp
Multi-language support beyond English (not in initial scope)
Complex authentication mechanisms beyond messaging channel verification
Offline mode or local data caching
Habit editing or modification after creation
Reminder delivery failure handling or retry mechanisms
initiativeCore.businessImpact: Success: Increased user engagement and retention through habit consistency features; potential for premium subscription revenue from advanced features. Failure: Low adoption if reminders are unreliable or integrations fail; user churn if progress tracking lacks value. Note: This is a proof-of-concept with simplified failure handling and authentication.
initiativeCore.stakeholders: Unknown - no stakeholders identified in brief

functional.description: Users can create and manage custom daily habits (maximum 5 habits per user)
Users can set and customize reminder schedules for each habit with flexible frequencies (daily, every 2 days, weekly, monthly, hourly intervals: 1h, 6h) and custom time-of-day schedules
System displays progress charts visualizing habit completion over time
System sends streak notifications when users maintain habit consistency
System integrates with SMS for bi-directional reminder delivery and habit completion
System integrates with Telegram for bi-directional reminder delivery and habit completion
System integrates with WhatsApp for bi-directional reminder delivery and habit completion
Users can mark habits as complete for each day via web interface
Users can define personal goals associated with habits
System tracks and displays current streak count per habit
Users can mark habits as complete by replying to reminder messages on SMS, Telegram, or WhatsApp
System enforces maximum limit of 5 habits per user
System supports multiple reminder frequency options: daily, every 2 days, weekly, monthly, every 1 hour, every 6 hours
System parses and processes incoming messages from users to mark habits complete
System sends confirmation messages when habits are marked complete via messaging platforms
Users must select one messaging channel (SMS, Telegram, or WhatsApp) during signup for receiving reminders
System supports English language only for all messages and user interactions
Habits can only be marked complete for the current day (no backdating or historical completion)
Users can set custom time-of-day schedules with up to 12 times per day
System implements messaging channel verification authentication: sends random 6-digit code to user's selected channel, user confirms code to complete signup and login
System prevents marking the same habit complete multiple times on the same day
Users must select a timezone during signup which the system uses for all reminder scheduling and time-based operations
System sends confirmation message in format '[Habit Name] Completed!' when habit is marked complete via messaging platform
System persists habit data and streak information in database for long-term storage and retrieval
Messaging channel selection is permanent and cannot be changed after signup
System accepts replies in format 'Habit (xxxx): done' where xxxx is the habit identifier to mark habits complete via messaging
System operates in real-time only with no offline mode or local data caching
System immediately cancels all pending reminders when a habit is deleted
System ignores replies to outdated reminder messages (messages sent before the most recent reminder for that habit)
Habits cannot be edited or modified after creation (no update functionality)
Users can stop a habit, which permanently cancels all future reminders for that habit
Stopped habits cannot be restarted; users must create a new habit if they want similar functionality
System does not implement reminder delivery retry mechanisms or failure handling
System generates and sends 6-digit verification code to user's selected messaging channel during signup
User confirms 6-digit verification code to complete signup and authentication
System authenticates user session after successful verification code confirmation

nonFunctional.description: Reminder delivery must be timely and reliable across all messaging platforms (best-effort, no retry on failure)
User interface must be intuitive and require minimal learning curve
Progress charts must load and render within 2 seconds
System must be accessible on mobile and desktop web browsers
Platform must handle concurrent users without performance degradation
User data must be securely stored and transmitted
Bi-directional message processing must occur within 5 seconds of user reply
System must handle message parsing errors gracefully and provide helpful feedback
6-digit verification codes must be securely generated and validated with appropriate expiration
All user-facing messages must be in English with clear, consistent language
All reminder times must be calculated and delivered according to user's selected timezone
Database must reliably persist habit and streak data with appropriate backup and recovery mechanisms
System must require active internet connection for all operations (no offline functionality)
Reminder cancellation must occur immediately upon habit deletion or stopping to prevent orphaned reminders
Verification code delivery must be reliable across all three messaging platforms
System accepts reminder delivery failures without retry as acceptable for POC scope

edgeCases: Messaging platform API is temporarily unavailable during scheduled reminder time (no retry, reminder lost)
User has multiple habits with overlapping reminder times
User deletes a habit with an active streak
User's phone number or messaging account becomes invalid
Streak calculation when user marks habit complete after midnight in their timezone
Chart rendering with incomplete or sparse habit data
User attempts to create duplicate habits
Rate limiting from messaging platform APIs during high-volume reminder periods
User attempts to create more than 5 habits
User replies with ambiguous or unrecognized text to mark habit complete
User marks same habit complete multiple times in same period
Reminder frequency conflicts (e.g., hourly reminders overlapping with daily reminders)
User replies to old reminder messages after new ones have been sent
Bi-directional message processing when webhook endpoints are down
Parsing replies in different languages or with typos
Handling rapid-fire replies from users across multiple habits
Monthly reminder scheduling across months with different day counts
User attempts to mark habit complete for past or future dates
Custom time schedules with times spanning across midnight in user's timezone
User sets overlapping custom times for the same habit
Verification code expiration during user signup flow
User attempts to mark already-completed habit as complete again on same day
User sets exactly 12 custom times and attempts to add more
User changes custom time schedule while reminders are pending
Timezone edge cases: DST transitions, timezone boundaries at midnight
Database connection failures during habit completion or streak updates
User signup with invalid or unsupported timezone
Confirmation message delivery failure after successful habit completion
User attempts to change messaging channel after signup (should be prevented)
Habit data corruption or inconsistency in database
Multiple simultaneous habit completions from different devices
Custom time schedule validation with times in different formats (12h vs 24h)
Reminder scheduling when user's timezone differs significantly from server timezone
User replies with incorrect habit identifier format (not matching 'Habit (xxxx): done')
User loses internet connection while marking habit complete via web interface
Pending reminders exist when habit is deleted (must be cancelled immediately)
User replies to reminder message that was sent before the most recent reminder for that habit
System receives reply to outdated reminder while newer reminder is pending
User deletes habit while reminder is in-flight but not yet delivered
Race condition between habit deletion and reminder delivery
User replies with valid format but non-existent habit identifier
Multiple reminders for same habit sent in quick succession due to custom time schedule
User attempts to use system offline or with intermittent connectivity
User attempts to edit or modify habit after creation (should be prevented)
User stops a habit and then attempts to restart it (should be prevented)
User stops a habit with pending reminders (all future reminders must be cancelled)
User creates new habit identical to previously stopped habit
Verification code delivery fails during signup
User enters incorrect verification code multiple times
User attempts to reuse expired verification code
Multiple signup attempts with same messaging channel identifier
User loses access to messaging channel after signup but before verification
Verification code sent but never confirmed (abandoned signup)
User attempts to verify with code sent to different messaging channel
Reminder delivery fails silently with no retry or user notification

criteria.description: User can successfully create a new habit with custom name and reminder schedule from available frequency options
User receives reminder notification via selected messaging platform at scheduled time according to their timezone
Progress chart displays accurate completion data for the past 30 days
Streak notification is sent when user completes habit for 3 consecutive days
User can mark a habit as complete and see updated streak count immediately
User interface is intuitive and easy to navigate
All three messaging platforms (SMS, Telegram, WhatsApp) successfully deliver reminders
User can customize reminder frequency and time for each habit independently using supported frequencies
App empowers users to cultivate positive habits effortlessly
User can reply to reminder message on SMS, Telegram, or WhatsApp to mark habit complete
System prevents user from creating more than 5 habits and displays appropriate error message
System correctly processes bi-directional messages and updates habit status within 5 seconds
User receives confirmation message in format '[Habit Name] Completed!' after successfully marking habit complete via messaging platform
System supports all specified reminder frequencies: daily, every 2 days, weekly, monthly, every 1 hour, every 6 hours
User must select one messaging channel during signup and cannot change it afterward
All system messages and user interface text are displayed in English only
System prevents marking habits complete for past or future dates, only allowing current day completion
User can set custom time-of-day schedule with up to 12 times per day and receive reminders at each specified time in their timezone
User receives 6-digit verification code via selected messaging channel during signup
System prevents duplicate completion of the same habit on the same day
User selects a timezone during signup and all reminders are scheduled according to that timezone
System enforces maximum of 12 custom times per habit and prevents adding more
Habit and streak data persists correctly in database and survives system restarts
System accepts and correctly processes replies in format 'Habit (xxxx): done' to mark habits complete
System requires active internet connection and displays appropriate error when offline
All pending reminders are immediately cancelled when a habit is deleted
System ignores replies to outdated reminder messages and only processes replies to the most recent reminder for each habit
System prevents editing or modifying habits after creation
User can stop a habit and all future reminders are permanently cancelled
System prevents restarting stopped habits
User can confirm 6-digit verification code and complete signup/authentication
System authenticates user session after successful verification code confirmation
System accepts reminder delivery failures without implementing retry mechanisms

measurableOutcomes: Percentage of users who maintain 7-day streaks
Reminder delivery success rate per messaging platform (best-effort, no retry)
User retention rate after 30 days
Average number of habits tracked per active user (target: 3-5)
Time to complete first habit creation flow
Chart load time performance metrics
Bi-directional message processing success rate
Percentage of habits marked complete via messaging vs web interface
Average response time for bi-directional message processing
Custom time schedule adoption rate (percentage of users using custom times vs preset frequencies)
Verification code delivery success rate across messaging platforms
Percentage of users utilizing custom time schedules vs preset frequencies
Database query performance for habit and streak retrieval
Confirmation message delivery success rate
Timezone-related reminder accuracy (percentage of reminders delivered within 1 minute of scheduled time)
Distribution of messaging channel selection at signup (SMS vs Telegram vs WhatsApp)
Average number of custom times set per habit (target: 2-6 out of maximum 12)
Reply format parsing success rate for 'Habit (xxxx): done' format
Percentage of replies rejected due to outdated reminder message
Reminder cancellation success rate upon habit deletion or stopping
Average time between habit deletion/stopping and complete reminder cancellation
Percentage of habits stopped vs deleted
Rate of new habit creation after stopping previous habits
Verification code confirmation success rate
Average time to complete signup and verification flow
Percentage of abandoned signups (verification code sent but not confirmed)
Reminder delivery failure rate (accepted as normal for POC)

Reminder scheduling and notification service supporting custom time schedules with timezone awareness
Chart rendering library or component
User authentication system with 6-digit verification code generation and validation
Mobile-responsive web design framework
Bi-directional message processing engine
Webhook infrastructure for receiving incoming messages
Message parsing and natural language processing logic (English only)
Habit limit enforcement logic (5 habits maximum)
Messaging channel selection logic during signup
Current-day-only completion validation logic
Custom time schedule parser and validator (maximum 12 times)
Duplicate completion prevention logic
Timezone selection and storage during signup
Timezone-aware reminder scheduling engine
Confirmation message formatter ('[Habit Name] Completed!' format)
Database schema for habit, streak, and user timezone data
Reply format parser for 'Habit (xxxx): done' pattern
Real-time connectivity validation and error handling
Reminder cancellation service triggered by habit deletion or stopping
Outdated message detection logic to ignore old reminder replies
Message timestamp tracking to determine most recent reminder per habit
Habit edit prevention logic
Habit stop functionality with permanent reminder cancellation
Stopped habit restart prevention logic
6-digit verification code generator
Verification code delivery service across all messaging platforms
Verification code validation and expiration logic
Session management after successful verification

externalDependencies.name: SMS Gateway Provider (e.g., Twilio, AWS SNS)
Messaging platform rate limits and quotas
SMS provider inbound message processing capabilities
Verification code delivery reliability across messaging platforms
Timezone database and library for accurate timezone handling
Internet connectivity for all user operations

factors: Critical sections missing: stakeholders, timeline, budget, constraints
No defined success metrics or KPIs beyond basic outcomes
No stakeholder identification or approval process
No timeline or delivery milestones specified
No budget constraints or resource allocation defined
High dependency on external messaging APIs with potential rate limits and costs
Bi-directional messaging adds complexity to webhook infrastructure and message parsing
No technical constraints or architecture decisions documented beyond verification code authentication and database
Unclear scope boundaries for MVP features beyond core functionality
No rollout or phasing strategy defined
Potential compliance requirements for SMS/messaging not addressed
Webhook reliability concerns for bi-directional communication
Cost implications of bi-directional SMS messaging not assessed
Custom time schedule complexity with up to 12 irregular intervals per habit
Timezone handling complexity across different user timezones and DST transitions
Database persistence requirements without defined backup/recovery strategy
Permanent messaging channel selection may frustrate users who want to switch
Real-time-only operation creates dependency on continuous internet connectivity
Reminder cancellation upon habit deletion or stopping requires robust cleanup logic to prevent orphaned reminders
Outdated message detection adds complexity to message processing pipeline
Specific reply format 'Habit (xxxx): done' may confuse users without clear instructions
No habit editing creates inflexibility for users who make mistakes during creation
Stopped habits cannot be restarted, forcing users to recreate similar habits
No reminder delivery retry mechanism means failed reminders are permanently lost
Verification code delivery failure blocks user signup completely
POC scope with simplified failure handling may lead to poor user experience
mitigations: Define clear MVP scope with prioritized features (5 habit limit reduces initial complexity)
Establish stakeholder review and approval process
Document technical architecture and API integration strategy for bi-directional messaging
Create detailed timeline with milestones and dependencies
Assess messaging platform costs including bi-directional SMS charges and rate limits before commitment
Define measurable success criteria and KPIs
Identify compliance requirements for messaging integrations
Establish fallback mechanisms for messaging platform failures and webhook downtime
Define budget for external API usage including inbound message processing
Implement robust message parsing with clear user instructions and error handling (English only simplifies parsing)
Design webhook infrastructure with retry logic and monitoring
Create simple, standardized reply formats to reduce parsing complexity
Implement rate limiting and queue management for bi-directional messages
Test bi-directional flows extensively across all three platforms before launch
Single channel per user reduces API complexity and costs
Current-day-only completion simplifies data model and reduces edge cases
Verification code authentication provides simple, secure signup flow without complex password management
Validate custom time schedules to prevent excessive reminder frequency (12 time maximum enforced)
English-only support reduces localization complexity and accelerates development
Timezone selection at signup simplifies ongoing timezone management
Standardized confirmation message format ('[Habit Name] Completed!') simplifies implementation
Database persistence provides reliable data storage with established backup patterns
Permanent channel selection eliminates channel-switching complexity and edge cases
Use established timezone libraries (e.g., moment-timezone, pytz) for reliable timezone handling
Implement database backup and recovery procedures following industry best practices
Document clear messaging channel selection UX to set user expectations at signup
Limit custom times to 12 to balance flexibility with system complexity and API costs
Test timezone edge cases thoroughly including DST transitions and midnight boundaries
Standardized reply format 'Habit (xxxx): done' provides clear parsing rules and reduces ambiguity
Real-time-only operation simplifies architecture by eliminating offline sync complexity
Implement immediate reminder cancellation on habit deletion/stopping to prevent orphaned reminders and user confusion
Track message timestamps to reliably detect and ignore outdated reminder replies
Display clear instructions in reminder messages showing exact reply format required
Implement connection status monitoring and user-friendly error messages for offline scenarios
Design reminder cancellation as atomic operation to prevent race conditions with in-flight reminders
Test outdated message detection thoroughly with various timing scenarios
Provide example reply format in onboarding and help documentation
No-edit policy simplifies data model and prevents complex update logic (acceptable for POC)
Stop-without-restart policy prevents complex state management (acceptable for POC)
Accept reminder delivery failures as normal for POC scope to simplify implementation
Implement verification code expiration to prevent security issues with old codes
Test verification code delivery across all platforms before launch
Provide clear error messages when verification fails
Document POC limitations clearly to set appropriate user expectations
Plan for future enhancements (habit editing, restart, retry logic) in post-POC phases

deliveryIntent.rolloutExpectations: Proof-of-concept with simplified failure handling and no retry mechanisms. Users should expect best-effort reminder delivery and limited flexibility (no habit editing, no restarting stopped habits).
deliveryIntent.timelineAssumptions: Unknown - no timeline provided in brief
constraints: Maximum 5 habits per user to limit initial complexity and API costs
Bi-directional messaging must be supported across all three platforms (SMS, Telegram, WhatsApp)
Reminder frequencies limited to: daily, every 2 days, weekly, monthly, every 1 hour, every 6 hours, plus custom time-of-day schedules
Social features explicitly excluded from initial scope
One messaging channel per user selected at signup (no channel switching allowed)
English language only for all messages and UI
No historical habit completion (current day only)
Verification code authentication only (6-digit code sent via messaging channel)
No backdating or future-dating of habit completions
Maximum 12 custom times per habit per day
User must select timezone during signup (used for all reminder scheduling)
Confirmation messages must follow format: '[Habit Name] Completed!'
Habit and streak data must be persisted in database
Messaging channel selection is permanent and cannot be changed after signup
Reply format must be 'Habit (xxxx): done' where xxxx is the habit identifier
Real-time operation only - no offline mode or local data caching
All pending reminders must be cancelled immediately when habit is deleted or stopped
System must ignore replies to outdated reminder messages (only process replies to most recent reminder per habit)
Habits cannot be edited or modified after creation
Stopped habits cannot be restarted (users must create new habits)
No reminder delivery retry mechanisms (failed reminders are lost)
POC scope accepts simplified failure handling as acceptable trade-off for faster development
deliveryIntent.budget: Unknown - no budget specified in brief
